interpretation: AMICA1, also known as Junctional Adhesion Molecule-Like (JAML), is a transmembrane protein involved in the regulation of leukocyte migration and activation. It interacts with the CXADR receptor, facilitating the activation of gamma-delta T-cells, which play a crucial role in tissue repair and homeostasis. In the context of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), the dysregulation of immune responses and inflammation is significant, as these factors contribute to the pathogenesis of the disease. However, the evidence linking AMICA1 directly to SONFH is limited, primarily suggesting its role in immune modulation rather than direct involvement in the disease process. Therefore, while AMICA1 may have potential as a biomarker due to its involvement in immune responses, the current evidence does not strongly support its clinical utility for early detection of SONFH.
relevance_summary: AMICA1 may play a role in immune modulation relevant to the pathogenesis of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH).
citations: AMICA1 — adhesion molecule that interacts with CXADR antigen 1 (NCBI Gene). https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/
JAML — junction adhesion molecule like (NCBI Gene). https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene/

result.title: JAML (Q86YT9)
result.text:
Protein: Junctional adhesion molecule-like
Function: Transmembrane protein of the plasma membrane of leukocytes that control their migration and activation through interaction with CXADR, a plasma membrane receptor found on adjacent epithelial and endothelial cells. The interaction between both receptors mediates the activation of gamma-delta T-cells, a subpopulation of T-cells residing in epithelia and involved in tissue homeostasis and repair. Upon epithelial CXADR-binding, JAML induces downstream cell signaling events in gamma-delta T-cells through PI3-kinase and MAP kinases. It results in proliferation and production of cytokines and growth factors by T-cells that in turn stimulate epithelial tissues repair. It also controls the transmigration of leukocytes within epithelial and endothelial tissues through adhesive interactions with epithelial and endothelial CXADR

result.title: AMICA1 ↔ lung adenocarcinoma
result.text:
Disease: lung adenocarcinoma
Description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. There is a male predilection with a male to female ratio of 2:1. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.
Association score: 0.269

result.title: AMICA1 ↔ placenta praevia
result.text:
Disease: placenta praevia
Description: Abnormal placentation in which the placenta implants in the lower segment of the uterus (the zone of dilation) and may cover part or all of the opening of the cervix. It is often associated with serious antepartum bleeding and premature labor.
Association score: 0.212

result.title: AMICA1 ↔ intracranial hemorrhage
result.text:
Disease: intracranial hemorrhage
Description: Bleeding within the SKULL, including hemorrhages in the brain and the three membranes of MENINGES. The escape of blood often leads to the formation of HEMATOMA in the cranial epidural, subdural, and subarachnoid spaces.
Association score: 0.205

result.title: AMICA1 ↔ lung carcinoma
result.text:
Disease: lung carcinoma
Description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the lung
Association score: 0.204

result.title: JAML — junction adhesion molecule like
result.text:
Official symbol: JAML
Gene name: junction adhesion molecule like
Aliases / synonyms: AMICA, AMICA1, CREA7-1, CREA7-4, Gm638
Organism: Homo sapiens
Gene summary: Enables integrin binding activity and protein homodimerization activity. Involved in heterophilic cell-cell adhesion via plasma membrane cell adhesion molecules and myeloid leukocyte migration. Located in bicellular tight junction; nucleoplasm; and plasma membrane. [provided by Alliance of Genome Resources, Jul 2025]
